config_files.notes: Key=value format without sections. Overwritten by the in-game settings menu. Edit only while Enshrouded is fully closed.

keys.effect: Render resolution scale as a percentage of screen resolution. Range 50-100.
keys.notes: Enshrouded is visually demanding with large procedural open-world areas. Resolution scaling is particularly effective here. DLSS and FSR are available in-game and supersede this key when active.
Shadows over the large open plains biomes are particularly expensive. Medium is a reliable quality/performance balance.

keys.effect: Foliage density and draw distance multiplier: 0=low, 1=medium, 2=high, 3=ultra.
keys.notes: High CPU cost in forest biomes. Medium is the recommended starting point for CPU-limited systems.

Volumetric fog in Enshrouded's Shroud areas (the game's central environmental mechanic) is GPU-expensive. Reducing improves performance in Shroud exploration without significantly reducing gameplay visibility.

manual_only_settings.recommendation: DLSS Quality on RTX hardware. FSR Quality on AMD. XeSS on Intel Arc.
16-player cap on performance hardware. Performance degrades with each additional player in the same region due to shared simulation load.
manual_only_settings.reason: Server settings are configured at world creation, not via graphics.cfg.

notes: Enshrouded is an Early Access title that reached 1.0 in 2025. Performance has improved significantly since the 2024 EA launch — older benchmark data may underrepresent current performance.

The Shroud (the game's signature environmental hazard) is a significant GPU cost area due to volumetric fog and particle effects. Performance in Shroud areas will be 10-30% lower than in clear-sky areas.
Enshrouded supports multiplayer co-op up to 24 players on a dedicated server. Player count directly affects server-side simulation performance.
